frameworks.name: Bayesian observer model
frameworks.one_line: subjective interval emerges from prior combined with likelihood for timing

frameworks.name: perceptual learning
frameworks.one_line: training-induced changes in timing accuracy and precision over days

core_question: How are mean accuracy and precision of interval timing recalibrated by experience, and do they follow the same learning dynamics?
key_assumptions: subjective time interval is set jointly by a prior and a likelihood
accuracy and precision are separable timing measures
implicit task with drifting invisible bar isolates interval representation
manipulations: multiple days of training on an implicit interval-timing task
range of physical time intervals tested
feedback applied at a specific trained interval
key_findings: accuracy showed feedback-driven instantaneous calibration with partial transfer near the trained interval
precision improved slowly over long term with no clear interval specificity
time course and specificity of accuracy and precision were decoupled
interpretations: a Bayesian observer model captures the dissociation between accuracy and precision dynamics
the prior width gradually shrinks over sessions while likelihoods remain stable
